original text:
◆Intelligent integration: Designed by professional industrial design team, it integrates solar panels, light source, controller and battery, and is convenient for purchasing, transportation, installation and maintenance.
◆ Patent appearance: The source code design team carefully created, appearance, high-end, fashion, atmosphere.
◆Intelligent sensing technology: the third generation of military sensing technology, microwave radar sensing. It has a patent for invention and adjusts the brightness of the lamp according to the moving object, which is more energy-saving and user-friendly.
◆Intelligent power adjustment: automatically judge the weather and reasonably plan the discharge law.
◆Intelligent design: embedded in the microcomputer control system, intelligent control of charging and discharging, multiple working modes, etc., making the whole system more efficient and energy efficient.
processed_text:
Intelligent integration: Designed by professional industrial design team, it integrates solar panels, light source, controller and battery, and is convenient for purchasing, transportation, installation and maintenance.
 Patent appearance: The source code design team carefully created, appearance, high-end, fashion, atmosphere.
Intelligent sensing technology: the third generation of military sensing technology, microwave radar sensing. It has a patent for invention and adjusts the brightness of the lamp according to the moving object, which is more energy-saving and user-friendly.
Intelligent power adjustment: automatically judge the weather and reasonably plan the discharge law.
Intelligent design: embedded in the microcomputer control system, intelligent control of charging and discharging, multiple working modes, etc., making the whole system more efficient and energy efficient.

Total Population 1911 to 1961: Population grew from 10,024 in 1911 to 19,155 in 1961.
Age Structure, in 5-year bands, from 1911 to 1961: In 1911, only 5.19 per cent of the population were aged 65 and over, but by 1961 12.77 per cent were.
Infant Mortality Rate 1911 to 1973: The highest rate recorded was 140. infant deaths per thousand live births in 1911, and the lowest was 8.98 in 1965.
BUXTON Preferred English F. Youngs, Local Administrative Units: Northern England (London: Royal Historical Society, 1991), p. 659.

BURBAGE CP Parish-level Unit 1894 1974 F. Youngs, Local Administrative Units: Northern England (London: Royal Historical Society, 1991), p. 69.

COTE HEATH LG_Ward Parish-level Unit 1961 Census of England and Wales, Table 3, 'Acreage, Population, Private Households and Dwellings', for 'LAA, Wards, CP in RD, Con, NT'.
